content.problemStatement:
Bob and Alice are having a lockout match between them. There are three problems in the contest worth AAA, BBB, and CCC points respectively. Only the first player to solve a problem gets points for that problem. It is impossible for Bob and Alice to solve a problem at the same time. Chef wants to know if there is any chance of a draw if Bob and Alice manage to solve all 333 problems. A draw occurs when both players end with equal number of points.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of a single line of input, three space separated integers AAA, BBB, and CCC.


Output Format
For each testcase, output YES if the match can end in a draw, and NO otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings "yEs", "yes", "Yes" and "YES" will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers A, B and C – the points of the three problems.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if it is possible for Bob and Alice to finish with equal total points, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: No number equals the sum of the other two, so draw impossible.\nTest case 2: 4 = 2 + 2, so Bob can take the 4‑point problem and Alice the two 2‑point problems, totals equal 4.\nTest case 3: No number equals the sum of the other two, draw impossible.
In the first case 2 = 1 + 1, draw possible. In the second case 10 = 3 + 7, draw possible.
No problem's points equal the sum of the other two, so a draw cannot be achieved.

solution.approach: Let S = A + B + C be the total points. A draw is possible iff there exists a subset of {A,B,C} whose sum equals S/2. With only three numbers the only way this can happen is when one of the numbers equals the sum of the other two (e.g., A = B + C). Therefore for each test case we simply check if A == B + C or B == A + C or C == A + B. If any condition holds, print YES, otherwise NO.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
